title: EV Charging Stations
description: Optional module to assess EV charging station physical security, safety interfaces, and operational integration.

module_questions.text: Is physical access to EV charging equipment components (e.g., enclosures, service panels, cabinets) restricted to authorized personnel?
Is video coverage implemented for EV charging stations and their immediate approaches to support incident detection and post-incident review?
Is adequate lighting implemented at EV charging station locations to support visibility during hours of darkness?
Is a user-accessible method provided to request assistance or report an emergency at EV charging station locations?
Is there a documented process to inspect EV charging stations for damage, tampering indicators, and unsafe conditions?
Are EV charging cables and connectors managed and maintained to reduce tampering opportunities and physical safety hazards?
Are responsibilities defined for coordinating with site management or service providers to restore safe operation following damage or disruption at EV charging station locations?

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Consider implementing adequate lighting at EV charging station locations to reduce opportunities for vandalism and theft and to support visibility during hours of darkness.

sources.label: Improving Public EV Charger Locations — GreenTech (March 12, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: An option for consideration is positioning EV charging stations to maintain clear sightlines from routinely occupied areas or routine patrol routes to reduce concealment and deter criminal activity.

sources.label: Public Space Design — Urban Planning Journal (July 18, 2023)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Consider providing a user-accessible method to request assistance or report emergencies at EV charging station locations to support timely response.

sources.label: Modern Security Solutions for EV Chargers — TechSecure (June 21, 2024)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: An option for consideration is incorporating physical tamper resistance for EV charging equipment components to deter unauthorized manipulation and reduce damage and safety hazards.

sources.label: How to Address the Increasing Threat of Cyberattacks on EV Charging Stations — Virta (May 14, 2025)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: Consider establishing routine inspection and maintenance processes for EV charging stations to identify damage, tampering indicators, and unsafe conditions.

sources.label: Charging Station Security Standards — Electric Vehicle Infrastructure Alliance (March 10, 2023)

module_ofcs.ofc_text: An option for consideration is briefing relevant staff on recognizing and reporting EV charging station tampering, damage, or unsafe conditions to support timely intervention.

sources.label: The Importance of Staff Training in EV Charger Security — ChargeLab (October 2, 2024)

risk_drivers.driver_text: Unauthorized access to EV charging systems may enable physical tampering or unsafe conditions at charging stations.
Payment skimming or data theft at EV charging stations may increase criminal targeting of charging locations and associated users.
